methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Vivodyne is a Series A-stage biotech and health company founded by Andrei Georgescu and Dan Huh, having raised $40M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 11 companies: 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage biotech and health firms, including series B references such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $40M total raised as a baseline proxy, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage advancement, yielding an $80M implied valuation. Peer-median triangulation across the 6 same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables generates a median valuation of $805M, with the peer sample ranging from $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 6.6x across the 3 public tickers) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures for the target.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $68M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $442M. This wide dispersion reflects inherent uncertainty: stage-progression conservatively anchors to realized capital, while peer-median relies on more mature Series B comparables. Key uncertainties include Vivodyne's revenue trajectory, clinical or commercial milestones achieved post-raise, and the appropriateness of Series B peer valuations for a Series A company. Calibration to nearer-stage private data or disclosed metrics would narrow the range materially.
